text: Kum. H. Supriya v. S. Krishnappa, High Court of Karnataka at Bengaluru, WP No.37035 of 2025 (NC:2026:KHC:7501), judgment dated 9 February 2026. Writ under Article 227 challenging order dated 17.11.2025 in O.S.No.7/2018 rejecting I.A.No.VIII by defendant No.3 to amend written statement under Order VI Rule 17 CPC and Section 151 CPC; amendment sought to incorporate subsequent events including matters in O.S.No.59/2021. Court relied on LIC v. Sanjeev Builders (2022) 16 SCC 1, Dinesh Goyal @ pappu v. Suman Agarwal (2024 INSC 726) and a coordinate Bench decision (Shri Mohammadrafi v. Bandenawaz W.P.108512/2025). Held that refusal to allow amendment "has occasioned failure of justice warranting interference" and that allowing amendment causes "no prejudice" as plaintiff can file rejoinder; petition allowed, impugned order set aside, amendment permitted and trial Court directed to dispose suit within one year.

metadata.case_name: Kum. H. Supriya vs S. Krishnappa
metadata.court: High Court of Karnataka at Bengaluru

text: The respondent No.1/plaintiff instituted O.S.No.7/2018 for specific performance of an alleged sale agreement dated 14.09.2018 against defendant Nos.1 to 3. The petitioner (defendant No.3) filed I.A.No.VIII during cross-examination of PW1 seeking permission under Order VI Rule 17 CPC to amend the written statement by adding paragraph 17A to incorporate subsequent events, including matters in O.S.No.59/2021. The trial Court rejected the amendment application on 17.11.2025 on the ground that the petitioner had not exercised due diligence in seeking amendment earlier. The petitioner challenged that order under Article 227 by filing this writ petition.

metadata.case_name: Kum. H. Supriya vs S. Krishnappa
metadata.court: High Court of Karnataka at Bengaluru

text: The High Court held that amendment should be permitted where the proposed pleading relates to "subsequent events that are alleged to have occurred after institution of the suit" which "obviously could not have been pleaded" at the time of filing the original written statement. Relying on precedents of the Apex Court and a co-ordinate Bench, the Court found that the trial Court's refusal to allow the amendment "has occasioned failure of justice warranting interference by this Court." The Court emphasized that allowing the amendment would not cause prejudice because "the plaintiff would have an opportunity to file rejoinder/reply to the amended written statement," and therefore the trial Court's conclusion was erroneous. Accordingly, the appropriate exercise of jurisdiction under Article 227 is to set aside the impugned order and permit the amendment to prevent injustice.

metadata.case_name: Kum. H. Supriya vs S. Krishnappa
metadata.court: High Court of Karnataka at Bengaluru

text: The writ petition is allowed; the impugned order dated 17.11.2025 rejecting I.A.No.VIII in O.S.No.7/2018 is set aside and I.A.No.VIII under Order VI Rule 17 CPC is allowed. Liberty is given to the plaintiff to file rejoinder/reply to the amended written statement and the trial Court is directed to dispose of the suit within one year; all rival contentions are kept open.

metadata.case_name: Kum. H. Supriya vs S. Krishnappa
metadata.court: High Court of Karnataka at Bengaluru

text: The petitioner (defendant No.3) argued that the proposed amendment relates to subsequent events that arose after the written statement was filed and therefore could not have been pleaded earlier; she sought amendment under Order VI Rule 17 read with Section 151 CPC. She contended that allowing the amendment would not prejudice the plaintiff because the plaintiff could file a rejoinder/reply to the amended pleading.

metadata.case_name: Kum. H. Supriya vs S. Krishnappa
metadata.court: High Court of Karnataka at Bengaluru

text: The respondent No.1 (plaintiff) opposed the amendment on the ground of delay and lack of due diligence, arguing that the petitioner should have sought amendment earlier and that permitting it at the stage of trial would be prejudicial. The trial Court accepted that position and rejected the amendment application.

ORAL ORDER
 2

Kum.H.Supriya vs S Krishnappa on 9 February, 2026
This petition by the defendant No.3 in O.S.No.7/2018 is directed against the impugned order dated
17.11.2025 passed by the Senior Civil Judge and JMFC, Holenarasipura, whereby the application
I.A.No.VIII filed by the petitioner- defendant No.3 NC: 2026:KHC:7501 HC-KAR seeking
permission to amend the written statement by incorporating additional paragraph No.17a after
paragraph 17 was rejected by the trial Court.
2. Heard learned counsel for the petitioner and learned counsel for the respondent No.1 and perused
the material on record. For the order proposed, service of notice to respondent Nos.2 and 3 are
dispensed with.
3. A perusal of the material on record will indicate that the respondent No.1-plaintiff instituted the
aforesaid suit against the petitioner - defendant No.1 and defendant Nos.2 and 3 for specific
performance of an alleged sale agreement dated 14.09.2018 relating to suit schedule immovable
property and for other reliefs. The defendants are contesting the said suit. The respondent No.1
having adduced evidence, and at the stage of cross-examination of PW1, the petitioner filed the
instant application seeking amendment of the written statement by incorporating certain
subsequent events in relation to suit in O.S.No.59/2021 pending on the file of the Senior Civil Judge,
Holenarasipura. The said application having been opposed by the plaintiff, the trial Court proceeded
to pass the NC: 2026:KHC:7501 HC-KAR impugned order rejecting the application, aggrieved by
which, the petitioner is before this Court by way of the present petition.
4. A perusal of the impugned order will indicate that the trial Court has rejected the application on
the ground that the petitioner had not exercised due diligence in not seeking amendment earlier
prior to filing the instant application. In this context, it is pertinent to note that the proposed
amendment relates to cert

metadata.case_name: Kum. H. Supriya vs S. Krishnappa
metadata.court: High Court of Karnataka at Bengaluru

text:
rejected the application on
the ground that the petitioner had not exercised due diligence in not seeking amendment earlier
prior to filing the instant application. In this context, it is pertinent to note that the proposed
amendment relates to certain subsequent events that are alleged to have occurred after institution of
the suit including institution of O.S.No.59/2021, which obviously could not have been pleaded by
the petitioner- defendant No.3 at the time of filing the written statement in the suit of the year 2018.
5. Under these circumstances, in the light of the principles enunciated by the Hon'ble Apex Court in
the cases of LIC v. Sanjeev Builders (P) Ltd. - (2022) 16 SCC 1, Dinesh Goyal @ pappu v. Suman
Agarwal (bindal) & ors. - 2024 INSC 726 and a co-ordinate Bench of this Court at Dharwad Bench in
the case of Shri Mohammadrafi and Anr., Vs. Bandenawaz and Ors
- W.P.108512/2025 dated 16.12.2025, I am of the considered opinion that the impugned order
passed by the trial Court refusing NC: 2026:KHC:7501 HC-KAR amendment has occasioned failure
of justice warranting interference by this Court in the present petition. At any rate, since the plaintiff
would have an opportunity to file rejoinder/reply to the amended written statement, no prejudice
can be said to have been caused to the plaintiff if the proposed amendment was allowed and failure
on the part of the trial Court to appreciate this has resulted in erroneous conclusion.
6. In the result, I pass the following:
ORDER
 3

Kum.H.Supriya vs S Krishnappa on 9 February, 2026
i) The petition is hereby allowed.
ii) The impugned order dated 17.11.2025 on I.A.No.VIII in O.S.No.7/2018 on the file
of Senior Civil Judge and JMFC, Holenarasipura, is hereby set aside.
iii) I.A.No.VIII under Order VI Rule 17 of CPC is hereby allowed.
iv) Liberty is reserved in favour of the respondent No.1-
plaintiff to file rejoinder/reply to the amended written statement.
NC: 2026:KHC:7501 HC-KAR
v) The trial Court is directed to dispose of the suit within a period of one year from the date of
receipt of a copy of this order.
vi) All rival contentions on all aspects of the matter are kept open and no opinion is expressed on th
